answers.f: anticlockwise through an angle 45°– θ 24 PART B Physics

answers.a: The expected numbers of atoms of X and Y are never equal.

Titanium metal can be extracted from titanium(IV) chloride, TiCl . 4 Titanium(IV) chloride is heated to 1000°C with either sodium or magnesium metal in an atmosphere of argon. Which one of the following statements is correct? (A values: Na = 23; Mg = 24; Cl = 35.5; Ti = 48) r

answers.a: A greater mass of magnesium chloride than sodium chloride is produced for each tonne of titanium made.
answers.b: The atmosphere of argon is used as a catalyst.
answers.c: In each reaction equation, the ratio of the reacting metal to titanium(IV) chloride is the same.
answers.d: Titanium is a weaker oxidising agent than either sodium or magnesium.

partDBiology.question: Ambrosia beetles carry fungal spores from tree to tree. The beetles make holes in the branches of trees and create a network of tunnels in the wood. The fungus grows along the walls of the tunnels providing the only food source for the beetles. The fungus can penetrate xylem vessels and block them, which eventually kills the tree. Which option correctly describes the relationship between these organisms?

answers.a: The beetle and the fungus have a mutualistic relationship.
answers.b: The beetle and the tree have a mutualistic relationship.
answers.c: The fungus is a parasite on the beetle.
answers.d: The fungus is the primary producer that the beetle feeds on.
answers.e: The tree is the primary producer that the beetle feeds on. 49 PART D Biology

In a mammal, an impulse travels along a neurone of length 45cm in 3.0 ×10–3 seconds. Transmission across the synapse to another neurone takes 4.0 ×10–10 seconds. The synapse has a width of 20nm. Which row correctly compares the speed of transmission across the synapse with the speed of transmission along the neurone, and what is the method of transmission across the synapse? speed of transmission across the method of transmission synapse compared to speed of across the synapse transmission along the neurone

answers.a: 300 times faster chemical, by active transport
answers.d: 3 times slower chemical, by active transport

partDBiology.question: Four blood cells were taken from a human: • one that was about to start cytokinesis • one actively producing antibodies • two that cannot use oxygen for respiration What is the total number of chromosomes present in all of the cells mentioned above? (Ignore mitochondrial DNA.)
